title: The evolution of agentic surfaces: building with Claude Managed Agents
preview: As model intelligence and agentic harnesses evolve, Claude Managed Agents allows teams to build and deploy agents in production environments reliably at scale. Here's why and how teams are using it.

blocks.english: As model intelligence and agentic harnesses evolve, Claude Managed Agents allows teams to build and deploy agents in production environments reliably at scale. Here's why and how teams are using it.
blocks.chinese: 随着模型智能和智能体框架的不断演进，Claude Managed Agents 使团队能够在生产环境中可靠、大规模地构建和部署智能体。以下是团队使用它的原因和方式。

blocks.english: Getting an agent into production takes more than a good prompt. The agent needs somewhere to run the code it writes, credentials to reach your data, observable sessions, and infrastructure that scales with usage. On the Applied AI team, we work at the intersection of product, research, and the customers building on Claude—and we see the same pattern repeatedly: infrastructure is what separates a prototype from a production agent. All too often, teams burn development cycles on security, state management, permissioning, and harness tuning.
blocks.chinese: 将智能体投入生产不仅仅需要一个好的提示词。智能体需要一个运行其所编写代码的环境、访问数据的凭证、可观测的会话，以及随使用量扩展的基础设施。在应用 AI 团队中，我们处于产品、研究和基于 Claude 构建的客户之间的交汇点——我们反复看到同样的模式：基础设施是区分原型与生产级智能体的关键。团队往往在安全、状态管理、权限控制和框架调优上消耗大量开发周期。

blocks.english: Claude Managed Agents, our suite of composable APIs for building and deploying production-grade agents, pairs an agent harness tuned for performance with production infrastructure, allowing teams to go from prototype to launch in days rather than months. In this post, we'll cover the evolution of Anthropic's agentic building blocks, why we built Claude Managed Agents, and how teams are using it in production today.
blocks.chinese: Claude Managed Agents 是我们用于构建和部署生产级智能体的可组合 API 套件，将经过性能优化的智能体框架与生产基础设施相结合，使团队能够在数天而非数月内从原型走向发布。在本文中，我们将介绍 Anthropic 智能体构建模块的演进、我们为何构建 Claude Managed Agents，以及团队如何在今天的生产环境中使用它。

blocks.english: Evolving the agent architecture
blocks.chinese: 智能体架构的演进

blocks.english: When we opened up Claude to developers in 2023, the API was deliberately simple: tokens in, tokens out. You sent a prompt, Claude returned a completion, and you built the harness and underlying infrastructure.
blocks.chinese: 2023 年我们向开发者开放 Claude 时，API 刻意设计得简单：输入 token，输出 token。你发送一个提示词，Claude 返回一个补全结果，你自行构建框架和底层基础设施。

blocks.english: The API grew steadily richer over the years, but the contract underneath never changed: one request, one model turn, and your application decides what happens next. For a long time, that was enough. Summarizing a document, classifying a support ticket, rewriting a block of text—the kind of work that fits comfortably in a single turn.
blocks.chinese: 多年来 API 不断丰富，但底层的交互契约始终未变：一个请求、一轮模型调用，然后由你的应用决定下一步。在很长一段时间内，这就足够了。总结文档、分类工单、重写文本——这些任务都适合在单轮交互中完成。

blocks.english: Over time, however, the tasks people wanted to hand off stopped fitting. They wanted Claude to carry a task all the way through, look something up, act on it, see what changed, and decide what to do next. And they wanted it to operate in the systems their work already ran on, like a codebase, internal wiki, or ticketing system.
blocks.chinese: 然而随着时间推移，人们希望委派的任务不再适合这种模式。他们希望 Claude 能够将一个任务从头到尾完成——查找信息、采取行动、查看变化、决定下一步。而且他们希望它能在现有工作系统中运行，如代码库、内部 Wiki 或工单系统。

blocks.english: With the API, turning Claude into an agent meant building your own loop: ask the model what to do, run the tool, feed the result back, and repeat. You were responsible for building and deploying the agent scaffolding, which may need tuning as models evolve. For agents that require full customization, this approach makes sense. For agentic workloads that are more predictable and less complex, optimizing harnesses as models and products evolved became tedious.
blocks.chinese: 通过 API 将 Claude 变成智能体意味着你需要自己构建循环：询问模型该做什么、运行工具、将结果反馈回去，然后重复。你需要负责构建和部署智能体脚手架，而且随着模型演进可能还需要调整。对于需要完全定制的智能体，这种方式是合理的。但对于更可预测、不太复杂的智能体工作负载，随着模型和产品演进而不断优化框架变得非常繁琐。

blocks.english: Claude Code, the agentic coding tool we launched in 2025 that lets Claude interact directly with your codebase, contained our own version of that harness: the loop, tool execution, subagents, context management, and rich capabilities that made it an effective agent. Developers naturally wanted similar harness machinery for their own agents across various domains.
blocks.chinese: Claude Code 是我们在 2025 年推出的智能体编码工具，让 Claude 直接与你的代码库交互，其中包含了我们自己版本的框架：循环、工具执行、子智能体、上下文管理和丰富的能力，使其成为一个高效的智能体。开发者自然希望在各个领域为自己的智能体构建类似的框架机制。

blocks.english: To enable teams to build agents on top of the Claude Code harness, we released Claude Agent SDK. Claude Agent SDK gives developers tools to build their own agents on the same machinery that runs Claude Code instead of maintaining a homegrown loop. For a lot of teams, this is when agents became practical: the harness arrived already tuned for Claude with infrastructure primitives and it kept improving as Claude Code did.
blocks.chinese: 为了让团队能基于 Claude Code 框架构建智能体，我们发布了 Claude Agent SDK。Claude Agent SDK 为开发者提供了在运行 Claude Code 的同一机制上构建自己智能体的工具，而无需维护自制的循环。对很多团队来说，这是智能体变得实用的转折点：框架已经为 Claude 调优并配备基础设施原语，而且会随 Claude Code 一起持续改进。

blocks.english: Even with a harness, though, deploying agents in production environments can be challenging for several reasons:
blocks.chinese: 然而即便有了框架，在生产环境中部署智能体仍面临多项挑战：

blocks.english: - **Hosting and scaling.** Where does the agent run, how long can a process stay alive for a multi-hour task, and what scales it when usage grows?
blocks.chinese: - **托管与扩展。** 智能体在哪里运行？一个进程能为持续数小时的任务存活多久？使用量增长时什么来负责扩展？

blocks.english: - **Session management.** Where does an agent's history and progress live? Can a run survive an interruption and resume unencumbered? Can you go back and inspect what happened in previous sessions?
blocks.chinese: - **会话管理。** 智能体的历史记录和进度存储在哪里？一次运行能否在中断后不受阻碍地恢复？能否回溯检查之前会话中发生的事情？

blocks.english: - **Filesystem management.** Doing real work means producing artifacts: editing code, writing files, building outputs. Where does the agent get a workspace to act on, and what happens to that workspace between runs?
blocks.chinese: - **文件系统管理。** 做实际工作意味着产出工件：编辑代码、写文件、构建输出。智能体从哪里获得工作空间？运行间隔中该工作空间会发生什么？

blocks.english: - **Execution isolation.** The code Claude writes has to execute somewhere. What's the blast radius if it's wrong, and what boundary would you actually trust in production?
blocks.chinese: - **执行隔离。** Claude 编写的代码必须在某处执行。如果出错，影响范围有多大？你在生产中真正信任的隔离边界是什么？

blocks.english: - **Credentials.** The agent needs access to your systems. How does it get that access without exposing proprietary information to the code it generates?
blocks.chinese: - **凭证管理。** 智能体需要访问你的系统。它如何在不向生成的代码暴露专有信息的情况下获得访问权限？

blocks.english: - **Observability.** When an agent works autonomously for an hour and does something surprising, can you reconstruct every step it took?
blocks.chinese: - **可观测性。** 当智能体自主工作一小时并做出令人意外的操作时，你能重建它采取的每一步吗？

blocks.english: With the Agent SDK, many elements of the aforementioned production infrastructure are provided through Claude Code's machinery. The agent gets a real filesystem to work in, session state is persisted locally or on external storage, and observability is exportable through OpenTelemetry into whatever monitoring stack you already run.
blocks.chinese: 通过 Agent SDK，上述生产基础设施的许多元素由 Claude Code 的机制提供。智能体获得一个真实文件系统来工作，会话状态持久化存储在本地或外部存储上，可观测性可通过 OpenTelemetry 导出到你已有的任何监控栈。

blocks.english: However, as teams increasingly built agents that moved out of local development into production, they needed a way to deploy them at scale and with managed infrastructure. And as models and their surrounding harnesses become more advanced–running longer, executing more code, touching more systems, and taking more actions– scaling, security, and sandboxing became more challenging.
blocks.chinese: 然而，随着团队越来越多地将智能体从本地开发推向生产环境，他们需要一种方式来大规模部署并使用托管基础设施。随着模型及其周边框架变得更加先进——运行时间更长、执行更多代码、接触更多系统、采取更多操作——扩展、安全和沙箱化变得更具挑战性。

blocks.english: Several of these hurdles stem from a common architectural choice: agent harnesses often run inside the same container as the filesystem it works on. A container has to spin up (paying a startup cost) before Claude can think, the agent along with code execution lives right next to your credentials, and when the container dies, the run dies with it.
blocks.chinese: 这些障碍中有很多源于一个常见的架构选择：智能体框架通常与其操作的文件系统运行在同一个容器中。容器必须先启动（产生启动成本）Claude 才能思考，智能体和代码执行与你的凭证紧邻，容器终止时运行也随之终止。

blocks.english: Managed Agents solves these problems by decoupling the brain from the hands. The harness that calls Claude runs separately from the sandbox where code executes, and the session–an append-only log of every model call, tool call, and result–connects the two. Claude can start reasoning before any container exists, the sandbox stays far away from your credentials, and a whole run can be reconstructed from its session at any point.
blocks.chinese: Managed Agents 通过将"大脑"与"双手"解耦来解决这些问题。调用 Claude 的框架与代码执行的沙箱分开运行，而会话——一个记录每次模型调用、工具调用和结果的仅追加日志——将两者连接起来。Claude 可以在任何容器存在之前就开始推理，沙箱远离你的凭证，完整的运行可以随时从会话中重建。

blocks.english: When and why to use Claude Managed Agents
blocks.chinese: 何时以及为何使用 Claude Managed Agents

blocks.english: When building with Managed Agents, users define the task, the tools, and the guardrails, and Anthropic runs the agent on our infrastructure and handles the agentic loop underneath: how to give an agent an execution environment to call tools, how to recover when something fails, multi-agent orchestration, and more.
blocks.chinese: 使用 Managed Agents 构建时，用户定义任务、工具和防护栏，Anthropic 在我们的基础设施上运行智能体并处理底层的智能体循环：如何为智能体提供执行环境来调用工具、失败时如何恢复、多智能体编排等。

blocks.english: When the harness doesn't evolve alongside model intelligence, the agent breaks down. On Claude Sonnet 4.5, an agent would rush to finish as it neared the end of its context, cutting work short rather than using the room it had left—a pattern called "context anxiety." Our fix was to add context resets to the harness, baking in an assumption that Claude needed help staying coherent near the limit. That assumption didn't survive the next model. On Claude Opus 4.5, the behavior was gone, and the resets we'd added were just overhead.
blocks.chinese: 当框架没有与模型智能同步演进时，智能体就会出现问题。在 Claude Sonnet 4.5 上，智能体在接近上下文末尾时会急于完成任务，缩短工作而不是利用剩余空间——这种模式被称为"上下文焦虑"。我们的修复方案是在框架中添加上下文重置，固化了一个假设：Claude 在接近上限时需要帮助来保持连贯。这个假设在下一个模型上就不成立了。在 Claude Opus 4.5 上，这种行为消失了，而我们添加的重置反而成了额外开销。

blocks.english: For most organizations, maintaining a harness is overhead that doesn't differentiate their product. Harnesses have to be tuned for certain model behaviors; primitives like compaction, tool execution, and caching works differently on Claude than other models. With Claude Managed Agents, the harness evolves alongside the model, allowing teams to focus on what will differentiate their agents: context management and domain expertise.
blocks.chinese: 对大多数组织而言，维护框架是一种不会带来产品差异化的开销。框架必须针对特定模型行为调优；压缩、工具执行和缓存等原语在 Claude 上的实现与其他模型不同。使用 Claude Managed Agents，框架与模型同步演进，让团队专注于能创造差异化的方面：上下文管理和领域专业知识。

blocks.english: To enable developers to configure the context and tools necessary to build effective agents, Managed Agents is built around three primary resources: agents, environments, and sessions. An agent is a configuration: a model, a prompt, a set of tools, and the guardrails around them. An environment is the execution context the agent runs in: the sandbox container, its networking rules, and the packages pre-installed in it, hosted on our cloud or on infrastructure you control. Each run is a session, which pairs an agent with an environment and gets its own isolated sandbox instance. Sessions persist their full event history, sandbox state, and outputs server-side, so long-running work can pause, resume cleanly, and be traced step by step after the fact. With Managed Agents, you can define an agent and an environment once, then run many sessions against the same configuration as your workload grows.
blocks.chinese: 为让开发者配置构建高效智能体所需的上下文和工具，Managed Agents 围绕三个核心资源构建：智能体（Agent）、环境（Environment）和会话（Session）。智能体是一个配置：一个模型、一个提示词、一组工具及其防护栏。环境是智能体运行的执行上下文：沙箱容器、网络规则和预安装的包，托管在我们的云上或你控制的基础设施上。每次运行是一个会话，将一个智能体与一个环境配对，并获得自己独立的沙箱实例。会话在服务端持久化完整的事件历史、沙箱状态和输出，因此长时间运行的工作可以暂停、干净地恢复，并在事后逐步追踪。使用 Managed Agents，你可以一次性定义智能体和环境，然后随着工作负载增长对同一配置运行多个会话。

blocks.english: Building for production and scale on Managed Agents
blocks.chinese: 基于 Managed Agents 构建生产级和可扩展应用

blocks.english: Within Applied AI, we see agents go from prototype to production both inside Anthropic and across our customers' systems, across coding, finance, support, legal, and a dozen other domains. This gives us a clear view of what separates a demo from a production-ready agent and where teams often get stuck.
blocks.chinese: 在应用 AI 团队中，我们见证了智能体从原型到生产的过程，无论是在 Anthropic 内部还是客户的系统中，涵盖编码、金融、客服、法律等十几个领域。这让我们清楚地看到什么将演示与生产就绪的智能体区分开来，以及团队常在哪里卡住。

blocks.english: Below, we share the most common reasons to build on a managed service like Claude Managed Agents:
blocks.chinese: 以下是我们总结的基于 Claude Managed Agents 等托管服务构建的最常见原因：

blocks.english: **1. Credentials are kept out of the sandbox.** When everything runs in one container, the code Claude generates sits right next to your credentials, so prompt injections could lead the model to leak a token by convincing the model to read its own environment. We can protect against this by setting up robust guardrails within the same container, but decoupling the architecture enables a much more secure approach by keeping credentials out of the sandbox entirely. Tokens for tools like MCPs, CLIs, and GitHub repos live in a separate vault, and a proxy fetches them and decrypts them only on demand. Managed Agents provides Vaults that handle credentials out-of-the-box, so you don't need to run your own secret store, transmit tokens on every call, or lose track of which end user an agent acted on behalf of. Vault credentials are protected with envelope encryption before storage, and retrieval requires a signed request token for verification.
blocks.chinese: **1. 凭证与沙箱隔离。** 当一切运行在同一个容器中时，Claude 生成的代码紧挨着你的凭证，提示注入可能说服模型读取自身环境从而泄露 token。我们可以通过在同一容器中设置健壮的防护栏来防范，但将架构解耦能提供更安全的方案——将凭证完全排除在沙箱之外。MCP、CLI 和 GitHub 仓库等工具的 token 存储在独立的保险库中，代理仅在需要时才获取和解密。Managed Agents 提供开箱即用的保险库（Vaults）来管理凭证，无需自行运行密钥存储、每次调用都传输 token，或丢失智能体代理的终端用户记录。保险库凭证在存储前使用信封加密保护，检索需要签名的请求令牌进行验证。

blocks.english: **2. Lower latency from eliminated sandbox overhead.** Latency is a metric that is top-of-mind for many enterprise teams, since users acutely feel when they're waiting for Claude to respond. Without the Managed Agents architecture, a container has to be spun up for every session, even the ones where the agent only needs to think and never runs a tool. That setup time is wasted, and the user feels it as a delay before the first response. With Managed Agents, Claude begins reasoning immediately while the environment spins up in parallel, and sessions that never run a tool skip the container entirely. This means the user sees the first token without waiting on container startup, and the environment is ready by the time the agent needs to run something. In our testing, that cut the time-to-first-token by roughly 60% in the median case (p50) and by over 90% in the slowest cases (p95).
blocks.chinese: **2. 消除沙箱开销带来的更低延迟。** 延迟是许多企业团队最关注的指标，因为用户在等待 Claude 响应时感受非常明显。在没有 Managed Agents 架构的情况下，每个会话都需要启动容器，即使智能体只需要思考而不需要运行工具。这段启动时间是浪费的，用户感受到的是首次响应前的延迟。使用 Managed Agents，Claude 立即开始推理，同时环境并行启动，不需要运行工具的会话完全跳过容器。这意味着用户无需等待容器启动就能看到第一个 token，而且当智能体需要运行什么时环境已经就绪。在我们的测试中，这将中位情况（p50）的首个 token 时间缩短了约 60%，最慢情况（p95）缩短了超过 90%。

blocks.english: **3. Reliable, persistent sessions that enable session management, observability, and memory.** Instead of request/response, Managed Agents thinks in terms of events. A session is an ongoing stream of events: every model call, tool call, and result, are appended to a log that lives outside the process running the agent. With this architecture, you get real-time updates as events stream in while the agent works, and you can resume any session later with no database or save-points to manage. History is preserved between interactions unless you delete the session, and when a session goes idle its container is checkpointed so you can pick up cleanly from where it paused. And because the whole run is already a record of events, observability and memory come with it: the Claude Developer Console offers a native visual timeline view of your agent sessions, and a debugging experience that allows you to examine any transcript in-depth. Managed Agents also comes with features like Memory and Dreaming that also use this session durability. Dreaming is a scheduled process that reviews your agent sessions and memory stores, extracts patterns, and curates memories so your agents improve over time. Dreaming refines memory between sessions so that it can improve from recurring mistakes and user preferences by reading from the persistent session logs.
blocks.chinese: **3. 可靠的持久化会话，支持会话管理、可观测性和记忆。** Managed Agents 不以请求/响应方式思考，而是以事件方式思考。一个会话是一个持续的事件流：每次模型调用、工具调用和结果都追加到一个独立于运行智能体进程的日志中。通过这种架构，你可以在智能体工作时实时获取事件流更新，并且可以随时恢复任何会话，无需管理数据库或保存点。除非你删除会话，交互间的历史记录会被保留，当会话空闲时其容器会被检查点保存，因此你可以从暂停处干净地恢复。而且因为整个运行本身就是一个事件记录，可观测性和记忆随之而来：Claude Developer Console 提供原生的可视化时间线视图来查看智能体会话，以及允许深入检查任何对话记录的调试体验。Managed Agents 还提供记忆（Memory）和梦境（Dreaming）等利用会话持久性的功能。梦境是一个定期运行的过程，它审查智能体会话和记忆存储、提取模式、整理记忆，使你的智能体随时间改进。梦境在会话间精炼记忆，通过读取持久化会话日志，从反复出现的错误和用户偏好中学习改进。

blocks.english: **4. Flexibility in Anthropic-managed or self-hosted cloud containers.** By default, with Managed Agents, you can delegate both orchestration and tool execution to Anthropic-managed cloud containers. This makes hosting and scaling simple and easy, delivering a faster path to production. Because the brain is decoupled from the hands in Managed Agents, the hands can live anywhere, including inside your Virtual Private Cloud (VPC). Thus, we also offer self-hosted sandboxes for teams that want control over tool execution, so the agent's code, filesystem, and network egress never leave their environment. We also provide MCP tunnels, which let you connect Claude to Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers that run inside your private network. So self-hosted sandboxes control where the agent's code executes, and MCP tunnels control how Anthropic reaches MCP servers in your network, giving you the ability to control exactly what stays inside your boundary.
blocks.chinese: **4. Anthropic 托管或自托管云容器的灵活性。** 默认情况下，使用 Managed Agents 可以将编排和工具执行都委托给 Anthropic 托管的云容器。这使托管和扩展变得简单便捷，提供更快的生产上线路径。由于 Managed Agents 中大脑与双手解耦，双手可以放在任何地方，包括你的虚拟私有云（VPC）内。因此，我们也为希望控制工具执行的团队提供自托管沙箱，使智能体的代码、文件系统和网络出口永远不会离开其环境。我们还提供 MCP 隧道，让你将 Claude 连接到运行在私有网络中的 Model Context Protocol（MCP）服务器。因此自托管沙箱控制智能体代码的执行位置，MCP 隧道控制 Anthropic 如何访问你网络中的 MCP 服务器，让你能够精确控制哪些内容保持在你的边界内。

blocks.english: The built-in observability console for Claude Managed Agents records every event, so you can scrub the timeline, open any step, and read its raw payload.
blocks.chinese: Claude Managed Agents 的内置可观测性控制台记录每个事件，你可以浏览时间线、打开任何步骤并读取其原始负载。

blocks.english: Beyond these features, additional capabilities include outcomes that let an agent grade its own work against a rubric, multiagent orchestration, permission policies, and webhooks.
blocks.chinese: 除这些功能外，还包括让智能体按评分标准自评工作成果、多智能体编排、权限策略和 Webhook 等附加能力。

blocks.english: How customers are building on Managed Agents today
blocks.chinese: 客户今天如何基于 Managed Agents 构建

blocks.english: Across industries, customers are already shipping agents in production with Claude Managed Agents. Here are a few examples:
blocks.chinese: 各行各业的客户已经在使用 Claude Managed Agents 将智能体投入生产。以下是一些示例：

blocks.english: - **Notion** runs its Custom Agents on Managed Agents: teams assign work to Claude straight from a task board, Claude picks up the docs, meeting notes, and connected data around each task, and the finished code, decks, and sites land back in the workspace for review. Dozens of tasks run in parallel, and their team has described an early prototype turning roughly twelve hours of work into twenty minutes.
blocks.chinese: - **Notion** 在 Managed Agents 上运行其自定义智能体：团队直接从任务面板将工作分配给 Claude，Claude 获取每个任务相关的文档、会议记录和关联数据，完成的代码、演示文稿和网站回到工作空间等待审核。数十个任务并行运行，他们的团队表示早期原型将大约十二小时的工作缩短为二十分钟。

blocks.english: - **Rakuten** used Managed Agents to ship specialist agents across product, sales, marketing, and finance, each live within about a week.
blocks.chinese: - **Rakuten** 使用 Managed Agents 在产品、销售、营销和财务领域部署专业智能体，每个都大约一周内上线。

blocks.english: - **Sentry** paired its Seer debugging agent with a Claude agent that writes the patch and opens the PR, built in weeks instead of months by a single engineer.
blocks.chinese: - **Sentry** 将其 Seer 调试智能体与编写补丁和创建 PR 的 Claude 智能体配对，由一名工程师在数周而非数月内完成构建。

blocks.english: - **Asana** built AI Teammates that pick up tasks inside projects, and **Atlassian** put developer agents into Jira workflows.
blocks.chinese: - **Asana** 构建了在项目中接取任务的 AI 队友，**Atlassian** 将开发者智能体集成到 Jira 工作流中。

blocks.english: Getting started with Claude Managed Agents
blocks.chinese: 开始使用 Claude Managed Agents

blocks.english: We built Managed Agents to make it as easy as possible to spin up agents through Claude Code and the Claude Developer Console at platform.claude.com. The Console's quickstart, for example, lets you start from an agent template or describe an agent in plain language, then turn it into a production-ready agent you can secure and deploy in minutes.
blocks.chinese: 我们构建 Managed Agents 是为了让你能通过 Claude Code 和 platform.claude.com 上的 Claude Developer Console 尽可能轻松地启动智能体。例如，Console 的快速入门让你从智能体模板开始或用自然语言描述智能体，然后在几分钟内将其转化为可安全部署的生产就绪智能体。

blocks.english: In Claude Code, the /claude-api skill is provided by default and provides Claude with detailed, up-to-date reference material for building applications on Claude Managed Agents. We highly recommend that you utilize it for the best practices on setting up your Managed Agents application. Get started by running /claude-api managed-agents-onboard for an interview-driven walkthrough for setting up a new Managed Agent from scratch.
blocks.chinese: 在 Claude Code 中，/claude-api 技能默认提供，为 Claude 提供了关于在 Claude Managed Agents 上构建应用的详细、最新参考资料。我们强烈建议你利用它来获取设置 Managed Agents 应用的最佳实践。运行 /claude-api managed-agents-onboard 即可通过交互式引导从零开始设置新的 Managed Agent。

blocks.english: The future of building managed agents
blocks.chinese: Managed Agents 的未来

blocks.english: As teams share what they're building with Managed Agents, we see that the time they used to spend on production infrastructure now goes to what differentiates their agents: managing context and tailoring the experience to users. Now, when a new model comes out, you update your agent to use it, rerun your evals, and ship the improvement without touching the architecture underneath.
blocks.chinese: 随着团队分享他们使用 Managed Agents 构建的成果，我们看到他们过去花在生产基础设施上的时间现在转向了能为智能体创造差异化的方面：管理上下文和为用户定制体验。现在，当新模型发布时，你只需更新智能体使用新模型、重新运行评估，就能发布改进，无需触碰底层架构。

blocks.english: We're excited to see what you build.
blocks.chinese: 我们期待看到你的构建成果。
